tasks.description: Stabilize row materialization in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts` so grouped boundary commits no longer drift: switch `rowsToConfig()` to a shared 4-decimal policy, serialize hold clips from one rounded start/end pair, and update the focused render-bounds regression to prove repeated interior-boundary commits preserve adjacency with no gaps/overlaps.

Add lost-pointer-capture teardown for unified resize sessions in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`: wire `onLostPointerCapture`, route it through the same `endResize()` cleanup path as pointer up/cancel, ensure interaction-end notification still fires, and extend `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` with a lost-capture regression that proves preview overrides clear and release behavior completes.

Finish the type/migration layer rollback to soft-tag only: remove deprecated `PinnedShotGroup.start` and `PinnedShotGroup.children`, expand `PinnedShotImageClipSnapshot` only as needed to store absolute restore bounds, add one-way legacy-group repair in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts`, and tighten migration/serialization tests so projection-shaped input loads as `clipIds` only and legacy fields are never re-emitted.

Rewrite pinned-group sync in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts` around live `clipIds` and row actions: remove compatibility-helper usage, diff `Shot.images[]` generation order against current clips, reuse/add/remove row actions directly, rebuild ordered `clipIds` with `orderClipIdsByAt()`, keep sync deferred during active interaction, and update the relevant pinned-group/shot-group tests to assert derived children/start come from live rows instead of legacy projection data.

Rewrite `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` for the soft-tag model: snapshot each image clip's absolute state into `imageClipSnapshot`, switch to a direct final-video clip with `clipIds = [videoClipId]`, restore images from the snapshot through one soft-tag rows commit, and update `useClipEditing.test.ts` to cover both switch directions under the new data shape.

Rewrite external shot drop in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts` so row actions and group creation are committed directly in soft-tag form, preserving correct clip positions and ordered `clipIds`, and update `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` to validate the real row/meta/group result rather than compatibility-shim behavior.

Replace the remaining `moveGroup()`-style grouped track-move commits in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` with atomic soft-tag row/meta/order/group updates, ensuring cross-track moves also update group `trackId` and per-clip row assignment consistently with live preview, and update `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to cover the new commit shape.

Delete the deprecated compatibility layer and finish test realignment: remove `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `moveGroup`, and `projectPinnedShotGroups` from `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts`; scrub remaining imports/usages; update `timeline-save-utils.test.ts` plus any remaining soft-tag test fixtures; remove diagnostic `console.log` calls for the listed tags; and verify cleanup with precise symbol greps that target the deleted fields/exports rather than broad `start`/`children` matches.

Run the full verification pass and do not stop on the first failure: create a short throwaway repro script inside the workspace that exercises the reported grouped-boundary drift and release/lost-capture symptoms, run it before and after the fixes, delete it afterward, then run the accepted validation sequence (`npx vitest run` targeted suites, grouped-flow suites, `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor`, and `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` filtered to `src/tools/video-editor/`), fixing any failures until the video-editor tree is clean.

watch_items: Keep scope inside `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor` only; leave unrelated repo failures untouched.
Treat the two user symptoms as hard gates, not proxies: interior-boundary commits must stay stable and resize/lost-capture release must clear preview state and unblock save/poll sync.
Do not reintroduce projection-shape writes anywhere after migration; runtime commits should stay on `clipIds` plus live row actions.
For hold clips, use one rounded start/end pair so `roundedStart + roundedHold == roundedEnd`; independent rounding of `at` and `hold` is the drift source.
Lost-pointer-capture cleanup should use the same teardown path as pointer up/cancel; avoid a parallel state machine.
Keep autosave and poll-sync defer semantics intact during active interaction and ensure they re-evaluate on interaction end.
When rewriting cross-track grouped moves, explicitly update pinned-group `trackId` alongside row/meta changes so overlays and menus do not point at stale tracks.
`PinnedShotImageClipSnapshot` should only grow enough to restore images without legacy `start`/`children`; avoid unrelated metadata expansion.

Use precise cleanup verification for legacy-field deletion: grep for `PinnedShotGroup.start`, `PinnedShotGroup.children`, and deleted export names, not raw `start` or `children`, because derived shot-group APIs still legitimately use those names.
Assume `translateGroupMembers()` is the intended base for grouped move rewrites unless the code proves otherwise.
Assume filtered `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` output is the accepted video-editor TS gate; do not add a new tsconfig as part of this work.
If lost-pointer-capture fires while a grouped commit is already settling, prefer the existing end-resize path and verify it does not double-commit or leave interaction state stuck.
Net line count in shot-group-related files should go down after shim deletion; avoid compensating abstraction churn that keeps the compatibility footprint alive.
Remove only the listed diagnostic `console.log` tags; do not paper over real errors by deleting unrelated warnings or logging that still matters.

sense_checks.question: Does repeated interior-boundary commit/rematerialize cycling preserve exact adjacency between neighboring clips with no inward jump, overlap, or gap opening?
If `lostpointercapture` fires mid-resize, do resize overrides clear, the resize session end, and interaction-end notification fire through the same path as pointer up/cancel?
After loading legacy projection-shaped pinned groups, are groups converted to ordered `clipIds` only, with deprecated `start`/`children` absent from the saved/emitted shape?
Does pinned-group sync now rebuild group membership from `Shot.images[]` generation order plus live rows, while deferring updates during active drag/resize interaction?
Can final-video switch and image restore round-trip using only soft-tag metadata and snapshot bounds, with group `clipIds` matching the actual committed clips in both directions?
Does dropping a shot create row actions and a soft-tag pinned group in one coherent commit, with clip positions and ordered `clipIds` matching the visible result?
Do all grouped cross-track move callsites update rows, per-clip track meta, group `trackId`, and ordered `clipIds` atomically so committed state matches live preview?
Are the five deprecated shim exports, the two legacy `PinnedShotGroup` fields, their imports/usages, and the listed diagnostic logs physically gone, with tests rewritten around soft-tag behavior?
Did the throwaway repro confirm the original bugs before the fix and stop reproducing after it, and do the full video-editor tests plus filtered TypeScript gate pass cleanly?

meta_commentary: Execute in the approved order because later hook rewrites depend on a stable commit path and a fully soft-tag type surface. Keep each mutation path honest: live preview and commit must use the same conceptual model, so avoid any shortcut that patches only pinned-group metadata without the corresponding rows/meta/order updates. Use `apply_patch` for edits, follow `CLAUDE.md`, and prefer tightening existing helpers over adding new layers. Treat migration as the only backward-compat entry point. When verifying deletion, search for exact symbols or type-property access patterns so legitimate derived `ShotGroup.start` / `ShotGroup.children` usage does not create false alarms. The final validation task is not a formality: if any targeted or broad suite fails, read the failure, fix it, rerun, and only finish once the reproduction script is gone, the deprecated surface is deleted, and the accepted commands are clean for `src/tools/video-editor`.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Fix rowsToConfig rounding and add adjacency regression
Add lost-pointer-capture resize teardown and regression
Remove legacy fields and add soft-tag migration coverage
Rewrite final-video/image switching around soft-tag snapshots
Rewrite external drop and grouped cross-track commit paths
Delete compatibility helpers and remaining legacy references
Rewrite failing tests for soft-tag behavior
Remove diagnostics and run acceptance gates

validation.completeness_notes: All 9 numbered plan steps are mapped to finalize tasks. Step 6 was intentionally split across `T6` and `T7` because external drop and grouped track management are separate production surfaces with separate tests. The final task includes the required throwaway reproduction script, targeted test commands, full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest sweep, and filtered TypeScript verification.
